title: DuckDB returns different results on different OS
description:
### What happens?

When running a simple statement on different OS Linux, Windows and MacOS the result can differ.
This frustrates unit tests.

### To Reproduce

```sql
SELECT ARRAY_DISTINCT(['A', 'A', 'B', NULL, NULL]) AS arr;
```

On my Linux and GitHub Linux, reliably I will get:
```
[B, A]
```

On my Windows VM and GitHub Windows, reliably I will get:
```
[A, B]
```

Although I have experimented already with all kind of configurations:
```java
        Properties info = new Properties();
        info.put("old_implicit_casting", "true");
        info.put("default_null_order", "NULLS FIRST");
        info.put("default_order", "ASC");
        info.put("memory_limit", "1GB");
        info.put("threads", "1");
```

### OS:

Any

### DuckDB Version:

1.0

### DuckDB Client:

Java

### Full Name:

Andreas Reichel

### Affiliation:

manticore-projects.com

### What is the latest build you tested with? If possible, we recommend testing with the latest nightly build.

I have tested with a stable release

### Did you include all relevant data sets for reproducing the issue?

Yes

### Did you include all code required to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have

### Did you include all relevant configuration (e.g., CPU architecture, Python version, Linux distribution) to reproduce the issue?

- [X] Yes, I have
poc: SELECT ARRAY_DISTINCT(['A', 'A', 'B', NULL, NULL]) AS arr;

SELECT list_sort(array_distinct(['A', 'A', 'B', NULL, NULL])) AS i
    ORDER BY i;
key_bug_features: Different results from ARRAY_DISTINCT on various operating systems
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE test_array_distinct (id INTEGER, values ARRAY<VARCHAR>);
INSERT INTO test_array_distinct VALUES (1, ['A', 'A', 'B', NULL, NULL]), (2, ['B', 'C', 'C', 'A', NULL]);
SELECT ARRAY_DISTINCT(values) AS distinct_values FROM test_array_distinct WHERE id = 1;
SELECT ARRAY_DISTINCT(ARRAY_CONCAT(values, ['D', 'D'])) AS distinct_values FROM test_array_distinct WHERE id = 2;
SELECT ARRAY_DISTINCT(ARRAY[1, 2, NULL, 2, NULL]) AS distinct_numbers;
